title: Pytest suite contains no executable assertions

evidence.quote:
except Exception as e:
    print('Script Error:', e)

except Exception as e:
    print('Script Error:', e)

reasoning: The owned files are presented as pytest tests, but none define test functions, fixtures, or assertions. smoke_test.py only runs its checks under __main__, which pytest will not execute during normal collection. test_api.py and test_api_v2.py run side-effect code at import time and swallow top-level failures by printing them, so API or database failures are not represented as test failures. This means the claimed test-suite feature does not reliably validate the application behavior it appears to cover.
reproduction: Run pytest against these files. There are no collected test items/assertions, and failures inside test_api.py/test_api_v2.py are caught and printed instead of failing the run.
recommendation: Convert these scripts into real pytest tests: move side-effect code into functions named test_*, use fixtures for API base URL and database connection, and replace print-only checks with assertions on HTTP status codes, JSON shape, and expected database/API relationships. Do not catch broad exceptions unless the test re-raises or explicitly fails.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: These files are the tests under review; their current structure is the reason failures are not enforced.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a pytest test that monkeypatches or fixtures the API/DB dependencies, forces an endpoint failure, and asserts pytest reports a failing test rather than only printing the error.
minimumFixScope: Refactor smoke_test.py, test_api.py, and test_api_v2.py into collected pytest test functions with assertions and failing exception behavior.
